meta.title: OlonJS — Contract Layer for the Agentic Web
meta.description: OlonJS is a TypeScript framework for building JSON-driven websites with a deterministic data contract. Every section is typed, validated, and structurally addressable.

data.headline: Contract Layer
data.body: AI agents are becoming operational actors in commerce, marketing, and support. They need more than content — they need a contract. OlonJS is the deterministic machine contract for websites: every site typed, structured, and addressable by design. No custom glue. No fragile integrations. Just a contract any agent can read and operate.

data.label: Why OlonJS
data.headline: A Meaningful Web
data.subline: Whole in itself, part of something greater.
data.body: Most web frameworks separate concerns across layers — data, UI, validation, metadata — with no shared contract between them. OlonJS inverts this: the JSON data structure is the contract. Every layer — rendering, editing, validation, machine access — is a deterministic projection of the same typed source. The result is a site that is structurally coherent by construction, not by convention. Every site built with OlonJS is a holon: complete in itself, intelligible to the network around it. The meaningful web doesn't happen all at once. It grows one site at a time.

pillars.title: The data is the contract
pillars.body: One typed JSON source. Every layer — UI, editor, agent, SEO — is a projection. No translation, no drift.

pillars.title: Every site is a holon
pillars.body: Autonomous, complete, structurally intelligible. Each site is whole in itself and part of a network that understands it.

pillars.title: Built to be generated
pillars.body: Every constraint is also an instruction. The spec is precise enough for AI agents to scaffold a fully compliant tenant from scratch.

data.label: Architecture
data.headline: Six governing protocols.
data.body: OlonJS is specified as a versioned set of architectural protocols. Each protocol is independently versioned and mandatory for compliant tenants.

protocols.name: Modular Type Registry
protocols.desc: Core exports an empty SectionDataRegistry. Tenants extend it via module augmentation. Full TypeScript inference across all section types at compile-time, zero Core changes.

protocols.name: Tenant Block Protocol
protocols.desc: Each section type is a self-contained capsule: View.tsx, schema.ts, types.ts, index.ts. Renderable, validatable, and ingestible by the engine without additional configuration.

protocols.name: JsonPages Site Protocol
protocols.desc: Deterministic file system ontology and CLI projection engine. config/ separates global governance from per-page content. Reproducible across every environment.

protocols.name: ICE Data Contract
protocols.desc: Mandatory data-jp-* DOM attributes bind every section to its data. Any consumer that can traverse the DOM can identify and operate any content node — human or agent.

protocols.name: Base Schema Fragments
protocols.desc: BaseSectionData and BaseArrayItem enforce anchor IDs and stable React keys across all capsules. The foundation that doesn't move so your content never drifts.

protocols.name: Path-Based Selection
protocols.desc: Every node has an address. Content selection uses strict root-to-leaf path semantics — unambiguous, stable, operable by any consumer that knows the contract.

data.label: Quick Example
data.headline: Two steps. One contract.
data.body: Scaffold a fully compliant tenant in under three minutes. Then read any page via the OlonJS protocol — from a browser, a script, or an AI agent.
data.note: Every OlonJS tenant exposes a machine-readable manifest at

steps.title: Scaffold a tenant
steps.code:
# Install the CLI
npm install -g @olonjs/cli

# Scaffold a new tenant
npx @olonjs/cli new tenant

steps.title: Read via OlonJS protocol

data.label: Get Started
data.headline: Three paths in.
data.body: Start with the Core package, scaffold a full tenant with the CLI, or deploy a working example in one click.

cards.title: Install Core
cards.body: The Core package is free and open — forever. The contract, the protocols, the CLI. No lock-in on the foundation.

cards.title: Scaffold a tenant
cards.body: The CLI scaffolds a fully compliant tenant from a canonical script. Same result on every machine, every run.

cards.title: Deploy a template
cards.body: Clone a working OlonJS tenant and deploy it with one click. Explore the full capsule structure in a real project.
